first_lines: RFQ 26-D-00017 Howard F. Curren Advanced Wastewater Treatment Plant (HFC
AWTP) Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) Migration and Fiber
Public Announcement in Compliance With Requirements of Section 287.055 Florida Statutes (Consultants’
Competitive Negotiation Act) Applicable Law, Executive Orders, Rules, Regulations, and The City’s Standard
objective of the project is to migrate the remaining facilities operating on HSQ software to the VTScada
software so that the entire plant is monitored and controlled through the VTScada software system.
The City has recently completed new SCADA and Process Control System (PCS) programming, configuration
and design standards.  The project will ensure that the plant’s monitoring and control systems meet these
Complete work needed to migrate monitoring and control capabilities to the VTScada software.
Document SCADA network diagrams and control system programming.
is informed same shall be subject to reuse in accordance with Section 287.055(10), Florida Statutes. Firm (i)
confirms it has read and is familiar with Section 119.071(3), Florida Statutes regarding certain building
plans, blueprints, schematic drawings, which depict the internal layout and structural elements of a
building, facility, or other structure owned or operated by the City or other agency that are per said section
exempt from Section 119.07(1), Florida Statutes and Section 24(a), Art. I of the Florida Constitution

Local Area Network (LAN): Made up of the connection media, networking components, and nearby
The HFC AWTP is permitted to treat 96 mgd of raw sewage with a Type I two-stage, high rate (pure oxygen
and conventional fine bubble aeration) activated sludge biological domestic wastewater treatment plant.
This plant is operated to reduce the Total Nitrogen (TN) in the effluent to advanced standards, using
During the workshops, the HFC AWTP Master Plan Improvements Dewatering Facility Replacement
project was used to create a template of the format the P&ID format. The instrumentation legend and
example P&IDs from that project are provided in Appendix C. The Tagging protocol will be different for
Instrument tags on the P&IDs will use standard ISA format bubbles, with the building acronym representing
the process area. Loops within a building will be numbered consecutively through the process flow path.
The instrument tag format will be:
The PLC instrument tagging shall follow the same format as P&IDs. Additionally, these tags shall include
any modifiers. The below signal tags correlate to the examples provided for the P&ID tags above.
PLC 1, PLC 9, and PLC 18 are considered weakest links as each of these are hubs that direct
communications from several PLCs back to this Main Pump Station network switch. Loss of
communication to these PLCs will impact the PLCs that are connected to them.
Patch panel at PLC 5 has a trunk cable back to the network rack patch panel
o One fiber cable that connects the network to PLC 4, PLC 37, and PLC7 as follows:
PLC 7 connects by fiber to a patch panel at PLC 37
Replacement of the Existing Fiber Cables: The existing fiber cables included in the network are past their
useful life having been installed several decades ago. If the cables were to be replaced, it would have to be
done with parallel new fiber cables that allow the existing network to function without interruption.
However, the 11 fiber cables entering through a conduit beneath the main patch panel in the Main Pump
Station Building network room leave the conduit with very little room to pass through additional fiber.
Although it’s possible to utilize MaxCell sleeves to snake new fiber cables through the one existing conduit,
During the installation of new fiber cables, the existing cables shall be left in place. It is recommended to
perform field investigations during the design phases to verify new fiber cable can to installed utilizing the
The historical data from HSQ is to be saved and made available in the VTScada system. This shall be
addressed in the Main Pump Station and HPO Reactor Improvements project currently under design. At a
minimum, data starting from the beginning of 2000 shall be transferred.
The design team will coordinate with the process integrator and the City to develop the software. City staff
All improvements shall adhere the following requirements:
New PLCs can connect directly to plant fiber network, they do not have to connect through a remote
Uninterruptible power supplies shall be an APC UPS without ethernet connections and with a
bypass switch for easy change out. Relays shall be used on UPS outputs to provide alarm when
Submerged service – Ensure manufacturer will guarantee expected operational

The Howard F. Curren Advanced Water Treatment Plant (HFC AWTP) owns and operates a Supervisory

PACSystems Rx3i and 90-30 series programmable logic controllers (PLCs) at the field level. General
Electric was acquired by Emerson in February, 2019. This document refers to GE equipment as GE to
Existing network diagrams are provided in the Appendix 1 of this evaluation report. Table 3-1

An important function to consider for PLC evaluation is to review the impact on functionality of the
Established and reputable PLC manufacturers have a proven track record of delivering quality products
and reliable support. Based on customer reviews, industry feedback, and the company's history to
gauge its product line implementation and commitment to customer satisfaction were used for
PLC requires routine maintenance and upkeeping, software upgrades and version controls are
necessary to mitigate PLC downtime. PLCs with tools to manage software version, notification
feature and license upgrades are ranked based on the available options.
The three PLC suppliers, GE (RX3i), Rockwell (Logix series), and Schineder (M540/M580) are all
reliable PLCs, and within each of the evaluation factors, no one supplier was always top ranked
rendering a final recommendation challenging. Therefore, matrix decision analysis using weighted
but not necessarily limited to, I/O

Howard F. Curren AWTP - SCADA System
Hazen and Sawyer Project No. 41077-013 Task 261
The Howard F. Curren Advanced Water Treatment Plant (HFC AWTP) owns and
operates a comprehensive Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) system. This system is
primarily composed of General Electric (GE) PAC System Rx3i and 90-30 series programmable logic
controllers (PLCs) at the field level, and VTScada by Trihedral at the SCADA/HMI level. SCADA
With the changes being implemented at the HFC AWTP, the City aims to select a new report creation
software to ensure efficient identification of plant performance and regulatory compliance. Given the
critical nature of these reports, it is vital that the chosen software integrates seamlessly with the existing
database systems and offers comprehensive functionality for data analysis and reporting.
The following software platforms were evaluated
DreamReport, Ignition, PI System with PIVision and XLReport
Inductive Automation, founded in 2003 and headquartered in Folsom, California, has over
20 years of experience in industrial automation. The company specializes in SCADA systems and
industrial automation software, with Fortune 500 customers among its client base. Their flagship
AVEVA, a British multinational software company, originated as the Computer-Aided
Design Centre in Cambridge in 1967, created by the UK Ministry of Technology and Cambridge
University. Over its 40+ years as an engineering software leader in the plant, power, and marine
SmartSights, founded in 1993 and headquartered in Austin, Texas, is known for
delivering advanced industrial reporting and data analysis solutions, including products like XLReport,
WIN911, and SyTech. The company, with a team of 50-200 employees according to LinkedIn, has a
The software must be able to pull data from the VTScada database without the user
having access to the VTScada system or database. This capability is crucial for a plant operator because it
ensures seamless integration and data flow without compromising the security and integrity of the
and compliance without worrying about potential cyber-attacks. Due to the impact of
plant safety this requirement it is given an importance factor of 5.
The software should be able to meet 21 CFR Part 11 compliance. This ensures that electronic records and
The software should be easy to integrate into The City’s HMI - VTScada. This integration
is crucial for a wastewater operator because VTScada is a widely used HMI system that provides real-
time monitoring and control of plant operations. By ensuring compatibility with VTScada, the software
The company must have representatives that are easy to reach out to and get in touch
with, the representatives must also be well versed with the software and its capabilities. The company
must have representatives that are easy to reach out to and get in touch with, and the representatives must
This section compares the relative advantages and disadvantages of each software platform considered.
The scalability of this software is more restrictive than other software platforms as the
number of tags and users are licensed, this means that for smaller installations there will
be an increase in cost for more tags being added but once a certain threshold is met then an unlimited tag
licensing option is available, this is the same case with the number of concurrent users that can create
reports. Even though the City’s tag licensing would fall under the unlimited category the more restrictive
DreamReport has the advantage of being NIST certified for cybersecurity. Wastewater
treatment is a critical part of everyday life that the environment and many people’s lives
depend on. Being certified for adequate cybersecurity standards ensures adequate safety and security is
being followed. DreamReport also can meet 21 CFR part 11 compliance requirements, this makes the
reports and findings trustworthy, reliable, and equivalent to paper records and handwritten signatures.
Ignition non cloud edition has an upfront cost of $24,337.50 with a yearly support cost of $4867.5. The
city is expected to spend a total of $29,205.00 on the first year, $53,542.50 for five years, $77,880 for ten
The platform can integrate with the HMI however that depends on how it was set up, if
the HMI was not set up in a way that it can integrate with Ignition then it might not
adequately integrate, because of this it scored a rank of 1 for the HMI integration category.
Ignition’s offering of a permanent license for the on-site server has regular security updates. The cloud
version of the server will be updated with the latest features and security updates. Support can be
wish to run a cloud server. This is a significant issue as this would prevent certain features
from being able to be used.
Although Ignition is self-reported to be NIST cybersecurity Ignition has not been certified by the NIST.
This is a consideration that must be made as The City would be reliant on Inductive Automation to
accurately report their compliance and this might prove to be a risk if Inductive Automation was not
The PI System and its required add on platforms have a yearly licensing cost of $174,126.62, with a 5
year estimated cost of $870,633.10, a 10 year estimated cost of $1,741,266.20, and a 15 year estimated
not indicate whether the system cost more for additional tags as the quote was created
with 100,000 tags and 5 concurrent users. However, due to the uncertainty of whether an
additional cost would be incurred to add more tags the platform scores a ranking of 2 in the scalability
The PI System is able to have custom calculations and tracking for KPIs with an expansive built in
which means that plant managers can more effectively manage password security and
Another advantage is the cybersecurity of the platform, following several high quality standards for both
In addition to this the large presence of the company and large number of clients across the world it
XLReport has a licensing cost of $7,850.00, this is for a 3 year subscription plan which includes a one
time $2000 scoping meeting, software installation services, validation and customized training cost. The
Specialized functions can be used, with custom scripting and SQL capabilities being
enabled to search and edit the database if there is a need to do so. This gives the
XLReport software platform a ranking of 2 for the specialized functions category.
XLReport can integrate with several different kinds of HMIs as it has a multitude of adapters after
considering the ranking of other categories, the software platform ranks 2 for the HMI integration
disadvantage is something that must be kept in mind due to the significant risk that an
unsecure application can pose to The City. In addition to this the Sales
Additionally XLReport has a licensing agreement that requires a license for the number of concurrent
users that are able to generate reports, this could be an issue if The City decides to increase the number of
users that are able to generate reports to increase facility flexibility as this would incur an additional cost
Based on the outcome of the decision matrix the best choice for report creation software would be Ocean
Data System’s DreamReport platform. Although other platforms had advantages over the DreamReport
platform the overall scores for the DreamReport platform were 205 for the weighted score and 52 for the

This is Volume 2 of the City of Tampa’s standards for Supervisory Control and Data
Acquisition (SCADA) and Process Control System (PCS) programming, configuration
and design standards for the City of Tampa referred to as “the City” or abbreviated as
Volume 2 addresses practices for development and programming of control processors
• Programmable logic controllers (PLCs) of various types
The specific software to be used is Studio 5000 Logix Designer, Version 36
New software requirements must be submitted to the City with suitable lead
time, a minimum of four weeks, for a security evaluation and loading. It is the
developer’s responsibility to ensure all of the required tools are in place prior to
LD is the preferred language for Boolean equations. This is also an acceptable
language for the development of functions and functional blocks.
FBD is a graphical, symbolic language based on the assembly of interconnected
functional elements. The logic is similar to ladder logic, with left to right, top to bottom
feedback for valve instructions, or programming speed feedback, motor
current, motor RPM scaling, and drive fault status for variable speed drive instructions.
This will be application-dependent, based on the availability of information and field I/O
Each LD rung must include a comment clearly describing its purpose and function. The
first rung of each routine or section of rungs should summarize the overall function of
the routine/section as well as the logic found on the rung.
FBD programming increases the readability of complex operations such as those with
multiple paths based on Boolean functions (AND/OR/NOT) or selection blocks as well
as intermediate calculations (sums and limiting functions).
Each subroutine of any type will be documented. A single “No Output” [NOP] instruction
or unique coil designated as “Comment Coil” on the first rung of routine (see example in
Figure 6 First rung NOP rung comments) shall be utilized to provide a location for
Figure 6 First Rung NOP Rung Comments

descriptions of the change. Specific documentation requirements and format are
contained in the City’s Management of Change Policy (Refer to Volume 1). At a
Consistent naming across systems and controllers facilitates the City’s technicians’
efforts during troubleshooting. As such, a series of standards has been developed to
PLC programming does not accept hyphens, so PLC tags must use underscores to be
compatible with the program. The tagging standards shall remain the same in the PLC
with the only difference being the use of underscores instead of hyphens. The figure
below shows the tagging as it will be used inside the PLC program.
The following sections outline the meaning of the various codes and reference
appropriate appendices for specifics. While the listings try to address all needs, there
will be times when a specific need is not addressed, such as a new area or instrument.
In those cases, suitable suggested additions should be submitted to the City prior to use

to use for equipment are outlined in Appendix A. Additional designations are to follow
the ISA standards as presented on instrument legends followed by “000” to be the same
Distinct equipment numbers shall be incremented sequentially based on multiple
equipment working as part of a system in parallel or series operation. Wherever
possible, the equipment number shall match the equipment descriptor or start with 01
and increment based on the number of matching loop number and type items in that
Each controller shall be given a unique name using the format and abbreviations
outlined in the City’s asset tag standards.

and Conventions Manual provides a complete listing of the sites, building code, loop
appended to the controller name utilizing the structure _r## where the hash
marks reflect the specific version number. The specifics of the version as well as the
reasoning for incrementing will be contained in the internal program documentation. An
o AC = Reference to the type of card, AC indicates voltage type.
o DI = Reference to type of input, DI indicates discrete input.
o MOD-04 = Indicates the module number.

This is the lowest tier of programming structure. As such the name must provide clarity
into the exact programming construct. Each routine is linked to a process area for fast

The basic definition of an alarm (per ISA-18.2, Alarm Management) is an audible and/or
visual indication to the operator that an equipment malfunction, process deviation or
other abnormal condition requires a response. This standard contains definitions that
the signal being monitored, analog or digital. All setpoints will be supplied
Alarms cannot be individually enabled/disabled. Equipment must be disabled in order
to disable the alarms associated to that piece of equipment. Otherwise, all the
The current scaled value is Above the configured
Figure 10 Recommended Alarm Rater per Operator Guidelines
It is critical that alarms be generated at a rate at which the operator can recognize the
alarm and address the cause. Control system acceptance requires the average alarm
rate per operator to fall within the predictive classification, number of operators for a
particular system will be confirmed at pre-programming workshop.
The City’s programs will be written utilizing LD, and FBD languages only. Any
exceptions must be submitted for approval following the procedures contained in the
City’s Standards Deviation Request Policy (Reference Volume 1, SCADA & PCS
FBD sheets should be limited in size to that which can be printed on an 11” x
Tasks provide the broadest level of organization. They also provide a level of execution
Function blocks contain a listing of the supplied function blocks for use in
programming. There is a corresponding graphical component discussed in Volume 3,
HMI Standards. These function blocks are not to be modified without first submitting the
suggested changes to the City for review and approval.
between modbus devices where the master block

Most modern controllers process I/O through complex interface modules that provide a
significant amount of additional information. In the case of analog values this can be
channel fault, over-range, under-range, and wire-break. Digital inputs can also provide
The physical data is required to be aggregated with the quality information
into one cohesive unit with a new unique name. This point or tag name is what will be
used in lieu of the physical I/O address and any/all of its quality
Programmers shall organize all I/O, (local, networked and messaged) into four
Module configurations are to be kept as close to manufacturer default (out of
the box) as possible. This is to facilitate the replacement of modules on failure at some
point in the future. In all cases ‘Hot Swappable’ modules are preferred.
Modules that support advanced features, such as the HART protocol, should adhere to
the spirit of the specification as much as possible. The ability to employ these additional
no alarming information will be generated. Scaling, beyond the conversion of
integers to reals, is not to be employed. All failure states will be discussed with the City
at pre-programming workshop to determine the proper course of action.
Messages array bits are each mapped to a variable where variables 0XXX (where XXX
is the unique variable number) are reserved for digital outputs, 1XXX (where XXX is the
The AOI interface contains a duality in its nature. From the programmer’s
perspective, this allows them to interact with the code-block in a regular manner. From
the graphical developer’s perspective, this presents a reliable interface to contained
data that simplifies their data management requirements.
This interface is a strictly enforced contract with all parties into what the contained data
Data is the foundation of what the controllers are utilizing. All controllers support and
supply atomic data types; examples are Booleans, integers, and real numbers. These
are the fundamental elements upon which control programs are built. Controllers also
natively support arrays of these basic types, providing a primitive complex type.
The controller maintains its program, data, and configuration through non-volatile
memory (NVM). This is memory that can exist in several forms, battery backed up RAM,
Communications to remote controllers will utilize comprehensive fault
monitoring of the status. The message execution complete and error results will be
monitored and be utilized as data quality status. Additionally, the data being transferred
will contain a watchdog register with each message packet.
This value will be used to drive a timer which indicates the information being read from
The communication protocol between remote controllers and the supervisory (SCADA)
system will be based on the DNP3 protocol as specified by DNP.ORG. This protocol
shall be only used on remote sites. The transport layer for communications is to be

The overview provides a bird’s eye view of the entire plant/facility and all sub-areas (unit

which the user can navigate to a specific sub-area or process unit. The user cannot
monitor or control any equipment belonging to the sub-areas from the overview screen.

HMI tags for controller referenced data will maintain the same encapsulated

In places where data is not contained in encapsulating structures, the HMI tag will still
The following are a series of best practices and limitations for tags. The differences in
tagging are in part based on the differences between PLC programming and the
limitations of VTScada. For example, PLC programming is restricted to using
underscores as dashes are not able to be used. In comparison dashes are able to be
All tags will inherit the area of the parent or may be individually declared. The area
structure specified ensures that as areas are created, that are sub-areas of a larger
element, the use of wild-card characters will allow for adequate filtering.

Digital values are also to be shown in bold, blue using easily understood words. In these
cases the values are to have a simple description included within a surrounding
rectangle. In the case where multiple digital values are to be indicated in a table the
surrounding rectangle may encompass all of the values.
In addition to the process values or conditions the alarm status is to be clearly indicated.
Operator editable values are similar to normal process values with the exception that
they are intended to be written to the controller in addition to being read. As such, these
values require security rights to enable interaction. When the operator has suitable

The OIT graphic symbol library will conform to the same standards and requirements as

Once an object is created, it can be reused by replacing its default linked variable with a

The system utilizes different faceplates, each corresponding to a specific data type in the
PLC. Refer to Section 5 for more information. For every PLC data type created, there is
an associated faceplate in the system as shown in Figure 40. The objects have been
named using the following structure: FP_DATATYPE.
Each macro is structured with conditional "if-else" statements to identify the selected
object and execute the appropriate code segment as shown in Figure 42.
Figure 42 Macro code segment for Open-Close Actuator No. 1
Every object in the OIT is linked to a Boolean variable. This variable determines whether
the object has been selected by the user, allowing the system to update the faceplate
with the relevant information. For example, in Figure 42 Macro code segment for Open-
Close Actuator No. 1, the code within the if-else statement will execute only if the tag
H_010_KV047_101_01 is set to “ON”. This tag is turned "ON" when the valve is selected
by the operator, as shown in Figure 43. These variables shall be previously created in the
OIT. Refer to Appendix A OIT mapping
The software leverages the String Table feature in EasyBuilder Pro to dynamically
manage and display information on the OIT as shown in Figure 44 String Table.
Specific tables have been pre-configured with the statuses of valves, motors, and other
associated macro. The macro also includes an ID variable whose value must correspond
to the ID in the string Table to ensure accurate information display and operation.

Equal Business Opportunity Evaluation Weighted Points: CCNA Proposal Guidelines
Under CCNA solicitations, proposers must submit to preconstruction Good Faith Efforts (GFE)
requirements covering the inclusion of City of Tampa-certified SLBE firms. Such inclusion shall be clearly
addressed and documented utilizing DMI 10, 20, and 50 Forms. Proof of certification shall include
copies of current certification certificates. This applies to ALL Phase 1 preconstruction design services.
